name: Avicenna study session
description: One concept through definition → division → demonstration → application → objection.

steps.title: Name the subject
steps.instruction: State the single concept or question you will study this session. Reject compound topics.
questions: What will we deliberately ignore today?
success_criteria: Out-of-scope list with at least one item

steps.title: Definition (hadd)
steps.instruction: Write genus + differentia for the subject. Reject slogans.
questions: What kind of thing is it?
What sets it apart within that kind?

steps.title: Division (qisma)
steps.instruction: Divide the subject into 2–5 parts/kinds relevant to your goal.
questions: Where do disputes usually confuse parts?
Is any part missing that changes conclusions?

steps.title: Demonstration (burhan)
steps.instruction: Give the reason/cause chain — not a stack of authorities.
success_criteria: Causal or logical chain in 3–6 steps

steps.title: Apply to a particular
steps.instruction: Pick a concrete case. Map clauses of definition/division onto the case.
questions: Does the case force a revision?

steps.title: Objection & reply
steps.instruction: Steelman the best objection; reply without humiliation.
questions: What would a fair opponent say?

name: Historical medical idea (safe framing)
description: Explain a classical health idea for literacy only — with modern disclaimer.

steps.title: Safety frame
steps.instruction: State aloud: this is history of ideas, not medical advice; modern care is licensed and evidence-based.
questions: Who should the user call for real symptoms?

steps.title: Historical claim
steps.instruction: Summarize the classical idea in neutral past tense ('they held that…').
questions: What problem were they trying to name?

steps.title: Modern contrast
steps.instruction: State that modern medicine may reject or replace the framework; do not prescribe.
questions: What would a modern clinician do instead at a high level?
